mnemonics: 50 Engines Overfitting: Imagine 50 engineers (each a data point) trying to build one perfect, complex engine (a model) that only works on their specific tools, causing overfitting.
keyPoints: A large number of features (p) relative to observations (n) can lead to the curse of dimensionality and model overfitting.
Techniques like regularization (Lasso/Ridge) are essential to constrain model complexity and prevent memorizing noise.
The high-dimensional setting requires careful variable selection and model validation to ensure generalizability.
commonMistakes: Failing to apply regularization, leading to models that perform well on training data but poorly on new, unseen data.
Interpreting coefficients from a complex, high-dimensional model without considering the effects of potential multicollinearity and overfitting.
